executive_summary: Today's cyber threat landscape (2026-03-09) shows 89 active intelligence items across 8 categories. 29 CRITICAL and 44 HIGH severity threats detected. Key threats: Amaranth-Dragon: Weaponizing CVE-2025-8088 for Targeted Espionage in Southeast Asia; New year, new sector: Targeting India's startup ecosystem; Compromised Routers, DNS, and a TDS Hidden in Aeza Networks. Security teams should prioritise patching actively exploited vulnerabilities and reviewing CISA KEV list for immediate action items.

sections.title: 🚨 CRITICAL: Actively Exploited Vulnerabilities
sections.text:
• CVE-2026-25108 — Soliton Systems K.K FileZen OS Command Injection Vulnerability
  Soliton Systems K.K FileZen contains an OS command injection vulnerability when an user logs-in to the affected product and sends a specially crafted HTTP request.
  🔗 https://www.cisa.gov/known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalog
  CVE: CVE-2026-25108
  ⚡ Action: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.

  Cisco SD-WAN CLI contains a path traversal vulnerability that could allow an authenticated local attacker to gain elevated privileges via improper access controls on commands within the application CL
  🔗 https://www.cisa.gov/known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalog
  CVE: CVE-2022-20775
  ⚡ Action: Please adhere to CISA’s guidelines to assess exposure and mitigate risks associated with Cisco SD-WAN devices as outlines in CISA’s Emergency Directive 26-03 (URL listed below in Notes) and CISA’s “Hunt & Hardening Guidance for Cisco SD-WAN Devices (URL listed below in Notes). Adhere to the applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are not available.

  🔗 https://www.cisa.gov/known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalog
  CVE: CVE-2026-20127

Adhere to the applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are not available.

• CVE-2026-22719 — Broadcom VMware Aria Operations Command Injection Vulnerability
  Broadcom VMware Aria Operations formerly known as vRealize Operations (vROps) contains a command injection vulnerability that allows an unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary commands, potentia
  🔗 https://www.cisa.gov/known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalog
  CVE: CVE-2026-22719
  ⚡ Action: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.

  Multiple Qualcomm chipsets contain a memory corruption vulnerability while using alignments for memory allocation.

items.title: CVE-2026-25108 — Soliton Systems K.K FileZen OS Command Injection Vulnerability
items.summary: Soliton Systems K.K FileZen contains an OS command injection vulnerability when an user logs-in to the affected product and sends a specially crafted HTTP request.
items.detail: Vendor: Soliton Systems K.K | Product: FileZen | Action required by: 2026-03-17
items.action: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.

items.title: CVE-2022-20775 — Cisco SD-WAN Path Traversal Vulnerability
items.summary: Cisco SD-WAN CLI contains a path traversal vulnerability that could allow an authenticated local attacker to gain elevated privileges via improper access controls on commands within the application CLI. A successful exploit could allow the attacker to execute arbitrary commands as the root user.
items.detail: Vendor: Cisco | Product: SD-WAN | Action required by: 2026-02-27
items.action: Please adhere to CISA’s guidelines to assess exposure and mitigate risks associated with Cisco SD-WAN devices as outlines in CISA’s Emergency Directive 26-03 (URL listed below in Notes) and CISA’s “Hunt & Hardening Guidance for Cisco SD-WAN Devices (URL listed below in Notes). Adhere to the applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are not available.

items.title: CVE-2026-20127 — Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager Authentication Bypass Vulnerability
items.summary: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller, formerly SD-WAN vSmart, and Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager, formerly SD-WAN vManage, contain an authentication bypass vulnerability could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to bypass authentication and obtain administrative privileges on an affected system. This vulnerability exists because the peering authentication mechanism in an affected system is not working properly. An attacker could exploit this vulnerability by sending crafted requests to an affected system. A successful exploit could allow the attacker to log in to an affected Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller as an internal, high-privileged, non-root user account. Using this account, the attacker could access NETCONF, which would then allow the attacker to manipulate network configuration for the SD-WAN fabric.
items.detail: Vendor: Cisco | Product: Catalyst SD-WAN Controller and Manager | Action required by: 2026-02-27
items.action: Please adhere to CISA’s guidelines to assess exposure and mitigate risks associated with Cisco SD-WAN devices as outlines in CISA’s Emergency Directive 26-03 (URL listed below in Notes) and CISA’s “Hunt & Hardening Guidance for Cisco SD-WAN Devices (URL listed below in Notes). Adhere to the applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are not available.

items.title: CVE-2026-22719 — Broadcom VMware Aria Operations Command Injection Vulnerability
items.summary: Broadcom VMware Aria Operations formerly known as vRealize Operations (vROps) contains a command injection vulnerability that allows an unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary commands, potentially leading to remote code execution during support‑assisted product migration.
items.detail: Vendor: Broadcom | Product: VMware Aria Operations | Action required by: 2026-03-24
items.action: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.

items.title: CVE-2026-21385 — Qualcomm Multiple Chipsets Memory Corruption Vulnerability
items.summary: Multiple Qualcomm chipsets contain a memory corruption vulnerability while using alignments for memory allocation.
items.detail: Vendor: Qualcomm | Product: Multiple Chipsets | Action required by: 2026-03-24
items.action: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.

items.summary: A Chinese threat actor, Amaranth-Dragon, has been conducting highly targeted cyber-espionage campaigns against government and law enforcement agencies in Southeast Asia throughout 2025. The group swiftly exploited the CVE-2025-8088 vulnerability in WinRAR to deliver malicious payloads, including a custom loader and the Havoc C2 Framework. Their operations demonstrate sophisticated tactics, includi

items.title: Compromised Routers, DNS, and a TDS Hidden in Aeza Networks
items.summary: A shadow DNS network and HTTP-based traffic distribution system (TDS) hosted in Aeza International, a sanctioned bulletproof hosting company, has been discovered. The system compromises routers, altering their DNS settings to use shadow resolvers. These resolvers selectively modify responses, directing users to malicious content. The TDS incorporates a clever DNS trick to evade detection by securi

items.title: Knife Cutting the Edge: Disclosing a China-nexus gateway-monitoring AitM framework
items.summary: Cisco Talos uncovered 'DKnife', a sophisticated gateway-monitoring and adversary-in-the-middle (AitM) framework comprising seven Linux-based implants. Used since 2019, DKnife performs deep-packet inspection, traffic manipulation, and malware delivery via routers and edge devices. It targets various devices, including PCs, mobile devices, and IoT, delivering ShadowPad and DarkNimbus backdoors. The

items.title: The Shadow Campaigns: Uncovering Global Espionage
items.summary: This investigation reveals a new cyberespionage group tracked as TGR-STA-1030, believed to be a state-aligned actor operating from Asia. Over the past year, the group has compromised government and critical infrastructure organizations in 37 countries, targeting ministries, law enforcement agencies, and departments related to economic, trade, and diplomatic functions. The group employs sophisticat
countries: United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland

sections.title: 🔓 New CVEs — Patch Immediately
sections.text:
• CVE-1999-0084 (CVSS 8.4) — Certain NFS servers allow users to use mknod to gain privileges by creating a wr
  Certain NFS servers allow users to use mknod to gain privileges by creating a writable kmem device and setting the UID to 0.
  🔗 https://exchange.xforce.ibmcloud.com/vulnerabilities/78
  CVE: CVE-1999-0084

• CVE-1999-0095 (CVSS 10.0) — The debug command in Sendmail is enabled, allowing attackers to execute commands
  The debug command in Sendmail is enabled, allowing attackers to execute commands as root.
  🔗 http://seclists.org/fulldisclosure/2019/Jun/16
  CVE: CVE-1999-0095

• CVE-1999-0082 (CVSS 10.0) — CWD ~root command in ftpd allows root access.
  CWD ~root command in ftpd allows root access.
  🔗 http://www.alw.nih.gov/Security/Docs/admin-guide-to-cracking.101.html
  CVE: CVE-1999-0082

• CVE-1999-1471 (CVSS 7.2) — Buffer overflow in passwd in BSD based operating systems 4.3 and earlier allows 
  Buffer overflow in passwd in BSD based operating systems 4.3 and earlier allows local users to gain root privileges by specifying a long shell or GECOS field.
  🔗 http://www.cert.org/advisories/CA-1989-01.html
  CVE: CVE-1999-1471

• CVE-1999-1122 (CVSS 4.6) — Vulnerability in restore in SunOS 4.0.3 and earlier allows local users to gain p
  Vulnerability in restore in SunOS 4.0.3 and earlier allows local users to gain privileges.
  🔗 http://www.cert.org/advisories/CA-1989-02.html
  CVE: CVE-1999-1122

• CVE-1999-1467 (CVSS 10.0) — Vulnerability in rcp on SunOS 4.0.x allows remote attackers from trusted hosts t
  Vulnerability in rcp on SunOS 4.0.x allows remote attackers from trusted hosts to execute arbitrary commands as root, possibly related to the configuration of the nobody user.
  🔗 http://www.cert.org/advisories/CA-1989-07.html
  CVE: CVE-1999-1467

items.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Qualcomm 0-Day, iOS Exploit Chains, AirSnitch Attack & Vibe-Coded Malware
items.summary:
Another week in cybersecurity. Another week of "you've got to be kidding me."
Attackers were busy. Defenders were busy. And somewhere in the middle, a whole lot of people had a very bad Monday morning. That's kind of just how it goes now.
The good news? There were some actual wins this week. Real ones. The kind where the good guys showed up, did the work, and made a dent. It doesn't always

items.title: Web Server Exploits and Mimikatz Used in Attacks Targeting Asian Critical Infrastructure
items.summary:
High-value organizations located in South, Southeast, and East Asia have been targeted by a Chinese threat actor as part of a years-long campaign.
The activity, which has targeted aviation, energy, government, law enforcement, pharmaceutical, technology, and telecommunications sectors, has been attributed by Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 to a previously undocumented threat activity group dubbed

raw_items.title: CVE-2021-22681 — Rockwell Multiple Products Insufficient Protected Credentials Vulnerability
raw_items.summary: Multiple Rockwell products contain an insufficient protected credentials vulnerability. Studio 5000 Logix Designer software may allow a key to be discovered. This key is used to verify Logix controllers are communicating with Rockwell Automation design software. If successfully exploited, this vulnerability could allow an unauthorized application to connect with Logix controllers. To leverage this vulnerability, an unauthorized user would require network access to the controller.
raw_items.detail: Vendor: Rockwell | Product: Multiple Products | Action required by: 2026-03-26
raw_items.action: Apply mitigations per vendor instructions, follow applicable BOD 22-01 guidance for cloud services, or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable.

raw_items.title: Middle East Conflict Fuels Opportunistic Cyber Attacks
raw_items.summary: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East has triggered a surge in cybercriminal activity. Over 8,000 newly registered domains with conflict-related keywords have been identified, many of which may be weaponized in future campaigns. Multiple cases of malicious activity have been observed, including targeted attacks using conflict-themed lures, deployment of the LOTUSLITE backdoor, fake news blogs le

raw_items.title: Mobile spyware campaign impersonates Israel's Red Alert rocket warning system
raw_items.summary: A targeted campaign has been identified distributing a trojanized version of the Red Alert rocket warning Android app to Israeli users via SMS messages impersonating official Home Front Command communications. The malicious app retains full rocket alert functionality while running malicious code in the background. It bypasses Android security checks through certificate spoofing and runtime manipul

raw_items.title: Remote Access Delivered Through Fake Zoom and Google Meet Calls
raw_items.summary: A campaign using fake Zoom and Google Meet pages to lure victims into fraudulent video calls has been identified. The attackers use these pages to deliver remote-access software. Multiple domains hosting identical fake meeting pages were discovered, with one domain previously linked to a ClickFix campaign. The fake interfaces show an active meeting with expected participants. When victims join, th

raw_items.title: An Investigation Into Years of Undetected Operations Targeting High-Value Sectors
raw_items.summary: Since 2020, a Chinese threat actor dubbed CL-UNK-1068 has been targeting high-value organizations across South, Southeast and East Asia, focusing on critical sectors like aviation, energy, government, and telecommunications. The group employs a diverse toolkit including custom malware, modified open-source utilities, and living-off-the-land binaries to maintain stealthy persistence. Their techniqu

raw_items.title: New A0Backdoor Linked to Teams Impersonation and Quick Assist Social Engineering
raw_items.summary: A new backdoor, dubbed A0Backdoor, has been discovered in connection with a campaign using email bombing and IT-support impersonation over Microsoft Teams to gain Quick Assist access. The malware's loader exhibits anti-sandbox evasion techniques, and the campaign's command-and-control has shifted to a covert DNS mail exchange-based channel. This activity is attributed to the threat group Blitz Bri

raw_items.title: Unmasking an Attack Chain of MuddyWater
raw_items.summary: An intrusion attributed to MuddyWater, an Iranian-linked APT, was identified in a customer environment. The attack involved initial access through RDP, establishing an SSH tunnel, and deploying malware via DLL side-loading. raw_items.title: Fake CleanMyMac site installs SHub Stealer and backdoors crypto wallets
raw_items.summary: A deceptive website impersonating CleanMyMac tricks users into installing SHub Stealer, a sophisticated macOS malware. The malware steals sensitive data, including passwords, browser data, cryptocurrency wallets, and Telegram sessions. It can also modify wallet apps to steal recovery phrases. The attack begins with users pasting a command into Terminal, which downloads and executes a malicious scr

raw_items.title: InstallFix: How attackers are weaponizing malvertized install guides
raw_items.summary: A new attack technique called InstallFix targets users by cloning popular developer tool installation pages and presenting malicious install commands. Attackers distribute these fake pages through Google Ads, exploiting users' trust in familiar 'curl to bash' installation methods.
